pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8) - Physical Properties section states 'Flash Point: 0°F (NTP, 1992)'
CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - states 'Flash point 40°F'
CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/3874) - Physical Properties section states 'Flash Point: 52°F (NTP, 1992)'

CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database: mixed xylenes (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8151) lists 81-115°F range; p-xylene isomer (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/9181) specifically lists 81°F
CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2272) - Physical Properties section states 'Flash Point: 104°F (NTP, 1992)'
CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8660) - Physical Properties section states 'Flash Point: 232°F (NTP, 1992)'
Task description specifies 100°F as cutoff for HIGH RISK vs LOWER RISK classification
Flash point 0°F < 100°F threshold = HIGH RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Flash point 40°F < 100°F threshold = HIGH RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Flash point 52°F < 100°F threshold = HIGH RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Flash point 81°F < 100°F threshold = HIGH RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Flash point 104°F >= 100°F threshold = LOWER RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Flash point 232°F >= 100°F threshold = LOWER RISK. Verified from CAMEO Chemicals data.
Chemical counts with flash point < 100°F: Acetone (0°F), Toluene (40°F), Methanol (52°F), Xylenes (81°F) = 4 total
Correct ranking by flash point from lowest (most hazardous) to highest (least hazardous): 0°F, 40°F, 52°F, 81°F, 104°F, 232°F
Task specification and setup_task.sh script (lines 17-20) confirm this output path

privileged_info_summary: The task requires looking up flash points for six chemicals on CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA database). Verified flash points from CAMEO: Acetone 0°F (contradicts metadata claim of -4°F), Toluene 40°F, Methanol 52°F, Xylenes 81°F (lower bound of 81-115°F range for mixed isomers), Acetic acid 104°F, and Ethylene glycol 232°F. The threshold of 100°F correctly classifies four chemicals as HIGH RISK (below threshold) and two as LOWER RISK (at or above threshold). The expected ranking from most hazardous to least hazardous (0°F → 40°F → 52°F → 81°F → 104°F → 232°F) is correct. The expected high-risk count of 4 is verified.
